    CS-404 Big Data Analytics

Week 08 – Graph Mining (PageRank)

         Dr. Syed Imran Ali

                                    25/03/2026
                                    Topics

• Graph Data
• Search Engines
• PageRank – Initial Idea
• PageRank – Improved Formulation
• Two Key Challenges
• PageRank – Google Formulation


* J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org

                                   Big Data Analytics
            Web Search: 2 Challenges

2 challenges of web search:
 (1) Web contains many sources of information
  Who to “trust”?
  Trick: Trustworthy pages may point to each other!
 (2) What is the “best” answer to query
 “newspaper”?
  No single right answer
  Trick: Pages that actually know about newspapers
   might all be pointing to many newspapers

                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
             Ranking Nodes on the Graph

• All web pages are not equally “important”
   www.joe-schmoe.com vs. www.stanford.edu



• There is large diversity
  in the web-graph
  node connectivity.
  Let’s rank the pages by
  the link structure!

                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
      PageRank:
The “Flow” Formulation




      CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                              Links as Votes

• Idea: Links as votes
   – Page is more important if it has more links
      • In-coming links? Out-going links?

• Think of in-links as votes:
   – www.stanford.edu has 23,400 in-links

   – www.joe-schmoe.com has 1 in-link


• Are all in-links are equal?
   – Links from important pages count more

   – Recursive question!
                           CS-895 Big Data Analytics
            Example: PageRank Scores



 A
                      B
3.3                                                                                                  C
                     38.4
                                                                                                    34.3




       D
                                                                E                                     F
      3.9
                                                               8.1                                   3.9


            1.6
                           1.6                                                            1.6              1.6
                                              1.6
                             CS-895 Big Data Analytics
            J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org
          Simple Recursive Formulation

 Each link’s vote is proportional to the
  importance of its source page

 If page j with importance rj has n out-links,
  each link gets rj / n votes

 Page j’s own importance is the sum of the
  votes on its in-links                                              i         k
                                                                         ri/3 r /4
                                                                               k

                                                                    j            rj/3
              rj = ri/3+rk/4
                                                                    rj/3       rj/3

             J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org
         PageRank: The “Flow” Model

 A “vote” from an important                                                                             y/2
  page is worth more                                                                                        y
 A page is important if it is
                                                                                             a/2
  pointed to by other important                                                                        y/2
  pages                                                                                    a
                                                                                                             m
                                                                                                                      m
 Define a “rank” rj for page j                                                                       a/2

                   ri
         rj                                                                              “Flow” equations:

              i j di
                                                                                                     ry = ry /2 + ra /2
                                                                                                     ra = ry /2 + rm
                                                                                                     rm = ra /2
        𝒅𝒊 … out-degree  of node 𝒊
                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
             J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org
            Solving the Flow Equations

 3 equations, 3 unknowns,                           ry = ry /2 + ra /2
 no constants                                        ra = ry /2 + rm
                                                     rm = ra /2
   No unique solution
   All solutions equivalent modulo the scale factor
 Additional constraint forces uniqueness:
   𝒓𝒚 + 𝒓𝒂 + 𝒓𝒎 = 𝟏
                  𝟐              𝟐               𝟏
   Solution: 𝒓𝒚 = , 𝒓𝒂         = , 𝒓𝒎         =
                  𝟓              𝟓               𝟓
 Gaussian elimination method works for
  small examples, but we need a better
  method for large web-size graphs
 We need a new formulation!
                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
      PageRank:
 The Matrix Formulation

(Stochastic Adjacency Matrix)




          CS-895 Big Data Analytics
           PageRank: Matrix Formulation

 Stochastic adjacency matrix 𝑴
   Let page 𝒊 has 𝒅𝒊 out-links
                              1
   If 𝒊 → 𝒋, then 𝑴𝒋𝒊 =          else             𝑴𝒋𝒊 = 0
                              𝑑𝑖
     𝑴 is a column stochastic matrix
       Columns sum to 1
 Rank vector 𝒓: vector with an entry per page
   𝑟𝑖 is the importance score of page 𝑖
   σ𝑖 𝑟𝑖 = 1
                                                                   ri
 The flow equations can be written                      rj  
                                                              i j d i
                     𝒓 = 𝑴⋅ 𝒓
                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                              Example
                                                        ri
 Remember the flow equation:
                                              rj  
                                                   i j d i
 Flow equation in the matrix form
                      𝑴⋅ 𝒓=𝒓
   Suppose page i links to 3 pages, including j
               i

       j                                            rj
                          .               =
                                     ri
 1/3

             M             .     r        =     r
         Example: Flow Equations & M


                                                   y     a   m
     y                                           y ½    ½    0
                                                 a ½    0    1
 a          m                                    m 0    ½    0

                                                       r = M∙r

ry = ry /2 + ra /2                                y   ½ ½ 0      y
ra = ry /2 + rm                                   a = ½ 0 1      a
rm = ra /2                                        m   0 ½ 0      m


                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                 Power Iteration Method


 Given a web graph with n nodes, where the

 nodes are pages and edges are hyperlinks
 Power iteration: a simple iterative scheme
                                                                        (t )
   Suppose there are N web pages                      rj
                                                          ( t 1)
                                                                  
                                                                        ri
                                                                   i j di
   Initialize: r(0) = [1/N,….,1/N]T

   Iterate: r(t+1) = M ∙ r(t)                     di …. out-degree of node i


   Stop when |r(t+1) – r(t)|1 < 
                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …

                             CS-895 Big Data Analytics
            J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org
         PageRank: The “Flow” Model

 A “vote” from an important                                                                             y/2
  page is worth more                                                                                        y
 A page is important if it is
                                                                                             a/2
  pointed to by other important                                                                        y/2
  pages                                                                                    a
                                                                                                             m
                                                                                                                      m
 Define a “rank” rj for page j                                                                       a/2

                   ri
         rj                                                                              “Flow” equations:

              i j di
                                                                                                     ry = ry /2 + ra /2
                                                                                                     ra = ry /2 + rm
                                                                                                     rm = ra /2
        𝒅𝒊 … out-degree  of node 𝒊
                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
             J. Leskovec, A. Rajaraman, J. Ullman: Mining of Massive Datasets, http://www.mmds.org
           PageRank: Matrix Formulation

 Stochastic adjacency matrix 𝑴
   Let page 𝒊 has 𝒅𝒊 out-links
                              1
   If 𝒊 → 𝒋, then 𝑴𝒋𝒊 =          else             𝑴𝒋𝒊 = 0
                              𝑑𝑖
     𝑴 is a column stochastic matrix
       Columns sum to 1
 Rank vector 𝒓: vector with an entry per page
   𝑟𝑖 is the importance score of page 𝑖
   σ𝑖 𝑟𝑖 = 1
                                                                   ri
 The flow equations can be written                      rj  
                                                              i j d i
                     𝒓 = 𝑴⋅ 𝒓
                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                Eigenvector Formulation

 The flow equations can be written
             𝒓 = 𝑴 ∙ 𝒓
 So the rank vector r is an eigenvector of the
 stochastic web matrix M
   In fact, its first or principal eigenvector,
    with corresponding eigenvalue 1                     NOTE: x is an
                                                        eigenvector with
     Largest eigenvalue of M is 1 since M is           the corresponding
                                                        eigenvalue λ if:
      column stochastic (with non-negative entries)        𝑨𝒙 = 𝝀𝒙
       We know r is unit length and each column of M
        sums to one, so 𝑴𝒓 ≤ 𝟏

 We can now efficiently solve for r!
 The method is called Power iteration
                        CS-895 Big Data Analytics
         Example: Flow Equations & M


                                                   y     a   m
     y                                           y ½    ½    0
                                                 a ½    0    1
 a          m                                    m 0    ½    0

                                                       r = M∙r

ry = ry /2 + ra /2                                y   ½ ½ 0      y
ra = ry /2 + rm                                   a = ½ 0 1      a
rm = ra /2                                        m   0 ½ 0      m


                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                 Power Iteration Method


 Given a web graph with n nodes, where the

 nodes are pages and edges are hyperlinks
 Power iteration: a simple iterative scheme
                                                                        (t )
   Suppose there are N web pages                      rj
                                                          ( t 1)
                                                                  
                                                                        ri
                                                                   i j di
   Initialize: r(0) = [1/N,….,1/N]T

   Iterate: r(t+1) = M ∙ r(t)                     di …. out-degree of node i


   Stop when |r(t+1) – r(t)|1 < 
                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
               PageRank: How to solve?

                                                        y   a     m
 Power Iteration:                       y
                                                 y      ½   ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1/N                                  a      ½   0      1
                 𝑟𝑖                  a       m   m      0   ½      0
  1: 𝑟′𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
                 𝑑𝑖
                                                  ry = ry /2 + ra /2
  2: 𝑟 = 𝑟′                                      ra = ry /2 + rm
  Goto 1                                         rm = ra /2

 Example:
 ry       1/3       1/3       5/12   9/24        6/15
 ra =     1/3       3/6       1/3    11/24 …     6/15
 rm       1/3       1/6       3/12   1/6         3/15
           Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
Random Walk Interpretation of the
      PageRank Algorithm




            CS-895 Big Data Analytics
             The Stationary Distribution

                                                i1       i2   i3
 Where is the surfer at time t+1?
   Follows a link uniformly at random
                                                     j
    𝒑 𝒕 + 𝟏 = 𝑴 ⋅ 𝒑(𝒕)             p (t  1)  M  p (t )
 Suppose the random walk reaches a state
  𝒑 𝒕 + 𝟏 = 𝑴 ⋅ 𝒑(𝒕) = 𝒑(𝒕)
  then 𝒑(𝒕) is stationary distribution of a random walk
 Our original rank vector 𝒓 satisfies 𝒓 = 𝑴 ⋅ 𝒓
   So, 𝒓 is a stationary distribution for
    the random walk

                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
            Existence and Uniqueness

 A central result from the theory of random
 walks (a.k.a. Markov processes):


   For graphs that satisfy certain conditions,
    the stationary distribution is unique and
 eventually will be reached no matter what the
   initial probability distribution at time t = 0


                  CS-895 Big Data Analytics
Google Formulation
                 PageRank: Three Questions

                      (t )
                      ri
                
      ( t 1)
 rj
                 i j di
                                     or
                                 equivalently     r  Mr
 Does this converge?

 Does it converge to what we want?

 Are results reasonable?

                      CS-895 Big Data Analytics
             Does this converge?


                                                                      (t )
                                                                      ri
                                                                
                                                      ( t 1)
             a                       b           rj
                                                                 i j di

 Example:
    ra       1          0           1        0
         =
    rb       0          1           0        1
             Iteration 0, 1, 2, …




                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
         Does it converge to what we want?



                                                                      (t )
                                                                      ri
                                                                
                                                      ( t 1)
             a                       b           rj
                                                                 i j di

 Example:
    ra       1          0           0        0
         =
    rb       0          1           0        0
              Iteration 0, 1, 2, …




                 CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                PageRank: Problems
                                                  Dead end
2 problems:
 (1) Some pages are
  dead ends (have no out-links)
   Random walk has “nowhere” to go to
   Such pages cause importance to “leak out”

 (2) Spider traps:
  (all out-links are within the group)
   Random walked gets “stuck” in a trap
   And eventually spider traps absorb all importance
                      Problem: Spider Traps

                                                                       y     a     m
 Power Iteration:                             y
                                                               y      ½      ½      0
   Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1                                                 a      ½      0      0
              𝑟𝑖                           a           m       m       0     ½      1
   𝑟𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
              𝑑𝑖
                                          m is a spider trap       ry = ry /2 + ra /2
      And iterate
                                                                   ra = ry /2
                                                                   rm = ra /2 + rm
 Example:
 ry            1/3       2/6       3/12    5/24                0
 ra =          1/3       1/6       2/12    3/24 …              0
 rm            1/3       3/6       7/12    16/24               1
                Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
 All the PageRank score gets “trapped”
                          CS-895 Big Data in node m.
                                         Analytics
                 Solution: Teleports!

 The Google solution for spider traps: At each
  time step, the random surfer has two options
   With prob. , follow a link at random
   With prob. 1-, jump to some random page
   Common values for  are in the range 0.8 to 0.9
 Surfer will teleport out of spider trap
  within a few time steps
                           y                       y

                       a              m        a       m
                   CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                       Problem: Dead Ends

                                                                    y     a     m
 Power Iteration:                            y
                                                             y     ½      ½      0
  Set 𝑟𝑗 = 1                                                 a    ½      0      0
             𝑟𝑖                           a           m      m      0     ½      0
  𝑟𝑗 = σ𝑖→𝑗
             𝑑𝑖
                                                                ry = ry /2 + ra /2
     And iterate
                                                                ra = ry /2
                                                                rm = ra /2
 Example:
 ry            1/3      2/6       3/12    5/24              0
 ra =          1/3      1/6       2/12    3/24     …        0
 rm            1/3      1/6       1/12    2/24              0
               Iteration 0, 1, 2, …
Here the PageRank “leaks”CS-895
                          out Big
                               since the
                                   Data   matrix is not stochastic.
                                        Analytics
                    Solution: Always Teleport!

 Teleports: Follow random teleport links with
 probability 1.0 from dead-ends
   Adjust matrix accordingly

            y                                y

        a             m              a               m
        y       a    m                   y       a   m
    y   ½       ½     0          y   ½           ½   ⅓
    a   ½       0     0          a   ½           0   ⅓
   m    0       ½     0          m       0       ½   ⅓
               Why Teleports Solve the
                     Problem?
Why are dead-ends and spider traps a problem
and why do teleports solve the problem?
 Spider-traps are not a problem, but with traps
  PageRank scores are not what we want
   Solution: Never get stuck in a spider trap by
    teleporting out of it in a finite number of steps
 Dead-ends are a problem
   The matrix is not column stochastic so our initial
    assumptions are not met
   Solution: Make matrix column stochastic by always
    teleporting when there is nowhere else to go
                     CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                  Solution: Random Teleports

 Google’s solution that does it all:
 Makes M stochastic, aperiodic, irreducible
  At each step, random surfer has two options:
   With probability , follow a link at random
   With probability 1-, jump to some random page

 PageRank equation [Brin-Page, 98]
                                                                        di … out-degree
                𝑟𝑖           1                                                 of node i
       𝑟𝑗 = ෍ 𝛽    + (1 − 𝛽)
                𝑑𝑖           𝑁
                  𝑖→𝑗
       This formulation assumes that 𝑴 has no dead ends. We can either
     preprocess matrix 𝑴 to remove all dead ends or explicitly follow random
                                CS-895
                teleport links with    Big Data Analytics
                                    probability 1.0 from dead-ends.
                    The Google Matrix
 PageRank equation [Brin-Page, ‘98]
                      𝑟𝑖            1
           𝑟𝑗 = ෍ 𝛽 + (1 − 𝛽)
                      𝑑𝑖            𝑁
                𝑖→𝑗
 The Google Matrix A:                 [1/N] …N by N matrix
                                                  NxN

                                1      where all entries are 1/N

           𝐴=𝛽𝑀+ 1−𝛽
                                𝑁 𝑁×𝑁
 A is stochastic, aperiodic and irreducible, so
         𝒓(𝒕+𝟏) = 𝑨 ⋅ 𝒓(𝒕)
And the Power method still works!
 What is  ?
   In practice  =0.8,0.9 (make 5 steps on avg., jump)
              Random Teleports ( = 0.8)

                                                    M                 [1/N]NxN
              0.8*1/2+0.2*1/3
          y                             1/2 1/2 0                   1/3 1/3 1/3
                                    0.8 1/2 0 0               + 0.2 1/3 1/3 1/3
                                         0 1/2 1                    1/3 1/3 1/3

                                                y 7/15 7/15 1/15
                        0.8+0.2*1/3
                                                a 7/15 1/15 1/15
a                                               m 1/15 7/15 13/15
                        m
                                                               A

    y         1/3    0.33 0.24            0.26                 7/33
    a =       1/3    0.20 0.20            0.18          ...    5/33
    m         1/3    0.46 0.52            0.56                21/33
                        CS-895 Big Data Analytics
              Random Teleports ( = 0.8)

                                                   M                 [1/N]NxN
              7/15
          y                            1/2 1/2 0                   1/3 1/3 1/3
                                   0.8 1/2 0 0               + 0.2 1/3 1/3 1/3
                                        0 1/2 1                    1/3 1/3 1/3

                                               y 7/15 7/15 1/15
                          13/15
                                               a 7/15 1/15 1/15
a                                              m 1/15 7/15 13/15
                      m
                                                              A

    y         1/3    0.33 0.24           0.26                 7/33
    a =       1/3    0.20 0.20           0.18          ...    5/33
    m         1/3    0.46 0.52           0.56                21/33
                       CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                  Matrix Formulation

• Suppose there are N pages
• Consider page i, with di out-links
• We have Mji = 1/|di| when i → j
       and Mji = 0 otherwise
• The random teleport is equivalent to:
  – Adding a teleport link from i to every other page
    and setting transition probability to (1-)/N
  – Reducing the probability of following each
    out-link from 1/|di| to /|di|
  – Equivalent: Tax each page a fraction (1-) of its
    score and redistribute evenly


                    CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                          Rearranging the Equation

                                                  𝟏−𝜷
   • 𝒓 = 𝑨 ⋅ 𝒓, where 𝑨𝒋𝒊 = 𝜷 𝑴𝒋𝒊 +
                                                   𝑵
   • 𝑟𝑗 = σ𝑁
           i=1 𝐴𝑗𝑖 ⋅ 𝑟𝑖
                            1−𝛽
   •   𝑟𝑗 = σ𝑁
             𝑖=1    𝛽 𝑀𝑗𝑖 +        ⋅ 𝑟𝑖
                             𝑁
                               1−𝛽 𝑁
             = σ𝑁
                i=1 𝛽 𝑀𝑗𝑖 ⋅ 𝑟𝑖 +   σi=1 𝑟𝑖
                                𝑁

                                                   since σ𝑟𝑖 = 1
                               1−𝛽
           = σ𝑁
              i=1 𝛽 𝑀𝑗𝑖 ⋅ 𝑟𝑖 +
                                𝑁
                                   𝟏−𝜷
   •   So we get: 𝒓 = 𝜷 𝑴 ⋅ 𝒓 +
                                    𝑵 𝑵




Note: Here we assumed M
has no dead-ends
                                          [x]N … a vector   of length N with all entries x
                              CS-895 Big Data Analytics
              Sparse Matrix Formulation

• We just rearranged the PageRank equation
                           𝟏−𝜷
              𝒓 = 𝜷𝑴 ⋅ 𝒓 +
                            𝑵 𝑵
     • where [(1-)/N]N is a vector with all N entries (1-)/N


• M is a sparse matrix! (with no dead-ends)
  – 10 links per node, approx 10N entries
• So in each iteration, we need to:
  – Compute rnew =  M ∙ rold
  – Add a constant value (1-)/N to each entry in rnew
     • Note if M contains dead-ends then σ𝒋 𝒓𝒏𝒆𝒘
                                             𝒋    < 𝟏 and
       we also have to renormalize rnew so that it sums to 1
                      CS-895 Big Data Analytics
                   PageRank: The Complete Algorithm

• Input: Graph 𝑮 and parameter 𝜷
    – Directed graph 𝑮 (can have spider traps and dead ends)
    – Parameter 𝜷
• Output: PageRank vector 𝒓𝒏𝒆𝒘
                         1
    – Set: 𝑟𝑗𝑜𝑙𝑑 =
                         𝑁
    – repeat until convergence: σ𝑗 𝑟𝑗𝑛𝑒𝑤 − 𝑟𝑗𝑜𝑙𝑑 > 𝜀
                                     𝒐𝒍𝒅
                                 𝒓𝒊
          •   ∀𝑗: 𝒓′𝒏𝒆𝒘
                    𝒋   = σ𝒊→𝒋 𝜷  𝒅      𝒊

                   𝒓′𝒏𝒆𝒘
                     𝒋   = 𝟎 if in-degree of 𝒋 is 0
          • Now re-insert the leaked PageRank:
                             𝒏𝒆𝒘    𝟏−𝑺
              ∀𝒋: 𝒓𝒏𝒆𝒘
                   𝒋   = 𝒓′ 𝒋      + 𝑵
                                               where: 𝑆 =
          • 𝒓𝒐𝒍𝒅 = 𝒓𝒏𝒆𝒘                        σ𝑗 𝑟′𝑗𝑛𝑒𝑤

If the graph has no dead-ends then the amount of leaked PageRank is 1-β. But since we have dead-ends
the amount of leaked PageRank mayCS-895     Big Data Analytics
                                     be larger. We have to explicitly account for it by computing S.
